titleStmt.title: Letter from Arthur Eagleston to the Under Secretary of Ireland, 4 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Arthur John Eagleston

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Arthur Eagleston (1870-1944) to the Under Secretary of Ireland regarding a petition of William Alexander of Belfast. Eagleston requests that the document be laid before the attorney general for Ireland in order to determine if there could be any objection to the document being endorsed in the usual manner.Arthur John Eagleston was a civil servant who served as assistant secretary of the British Home Office from 1913 to 1932. At the time this letter was written Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) was serving as the under secretary of Ireland. Chalmers was sent to Ireland as a replacement for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5618/11540

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HOME OFFICE, WHITEHALL. 4th July, 1916. PRESSING. Sir, I am directed by the Secretary of State to
forward herewith, the Petition of Right of Mr. William Alexander
of 5, Lombard Street, Belfast, together with a copy of
a letter from the War Office with reference thereto; and
I am to request that the document may be referred to
His Majesty's Attorney-General for Ireland for the favour
of his report whether there is any objection to the
document being endorsed in the usual manner 'Let Right
be done'. A copy of a letter from the Petitioner's Solicitors
is also enclosed. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, A.J. Eagleston The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle.
